title: ExecutionGrant
description:
An execution grant authorizating exactly one action on one connector.

This is a short-lived, action-specific token (typically 30s TTL). Connectors MUST validate this grant instead of accepting broad session JWTs. This is the key architectural change that makes scoped delegation real.

action_id.description: The action this grant authorizes (must match `ActionRequest.action_id`).

allowed_action.description: Exact action name this grant allows (e.g. "google.calendar.event.create"). The connector MUST verify this matches the requested action.

clearance.description: How this grant was obtained (auto-approved, human-approved, proof-verified).

expires_at.description: Expiry timestamp (Unix epoch seconds). Typically now + 30s.

kid.description: Key ID of the signing key. Used for key rotation — verifiers resolve the correct public key by this ID.

requester_did.description: DID of the entity that requested the action.

GrantClearance.description: How an execution grant was obtained — tracks the provenance of the authorization decision.

oneOf.description: Tier 1: human clicked "approve" in the portal.
Tier 3: human presented a verifiable credential (OID4VP).
